titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Jacqueline de Jong Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 10123126.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Sunday April 22, 2018 at 21:58

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

langmaterial.language: In Dutch, German, French, and English.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jacqueline de Jong on the Edith and Richard French Fund and the Frederick W. Hilles Fund, 2011-2013.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Jacqueline de Jong Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jacqueline de Jong Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Jacqeline de Jong
bioghist.p: Jacqueline de Jong (1939-), is a Dutch artist. She was associated with the social, political, and artistic movements Internationale situationniste and Gruppe SPUR during the 1960s, and served as editor of the Situationist Times from 1962-1968.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings: October 2011 Acquisition; May 2012 Acquisition; and November 2013 Acquisition.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below, titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, artwork, photographs and other papers relating to the life and work of Jacqueline de Jong during the 1960s. The papers include extensive correspondence and other papers documenting the Situationist Times, the Internationale situationniste, and Gruppe SPUR. Correspondents include Pierre Alechinsky, Guy Atkins, Max Bucaille, Pol Bury, Lothar Fischer, Dieter Kunzelmann, Daniel Spoerri, and Hans-Peter Zimmer. Also includes correspondence, writings, and other papers by Guy Debord and Asger Jorn.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged roughly in the order in which it was received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes original materials for Situationist Times no. 1, including manuscripts, ink drawings, and collages by Noël Arnaud, Rodolphe Gasché, J. V. Martin, Gruppe SPUR, and Jacqueline de Jong (including "Critique on the Political Praxis of Detournement"); maquettes; original copy of the issue.

did.unittitle: Documents relating to the initial period of the Situationist Times

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters to Jacqueline de Jong from Rodolpe Gasché; original copy of Mutant ; documents related to Peter Schat's "Entelechie II."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes draft of article on Gruppe SPUR trial by Rodolpe Gasché; drafts musical score and lyrics for "Le Prisonnier" by Boris Vian; original ink drawings by Theo Wolvecamp, Jacqueline de Jong, and Serge Vandercam; letter from Noël Arnaud; original copy of the issue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes complete maquette; layout mock-ups; original drawings; cut photo proof sheet; corrected drafts of articles by Anton Ehrenzwig, Max Bucaille, George Hay, and de Jong; collage by Max Bucaille; original copy of the issue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes index; corrected drafts of articles by H. C. Doets, Gordon Fazakerley, Hans L.C. Jaffé, Edward Mazman, Peter Schat, Lech Tomaszewski, and Aldo van Eyck; original slides and photographs reproduced in the issue; recipient addresses for distribution; several letters to de Jong; original copy of the issue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes maquettes; original photographs and found material reproduced in this issue; original copy of the issue; cut photo sheet; draft of Jacqueline de Jong's introduction to the issue; distribution lists; correspondence related to the publication of this issue; Rhodos poster; manuscript of H.C. Doets article and correspondence related to its publication; manuscripts and English translations of Asger Jorn's "Art and Order" and "Mind and Sense"; manuscript of Max Bucaille article; manuscript of Lech Tomaszewski article and correspondence related to its publication; manuscript and figures or David George Emmerich article and correspondence related to its publication; and ICA Bulletin addendum to this issue containing the censored paragraphs from "Kreise, Kreissegmente und Wellenlinien."

did.unittitle: Papers relating to Situationist Times nos. 4, 5, and 7

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, primarily letters and postcards from individuals involved with the Situationist Times, including Noël Arnaud, Max Bucaille, David Georges Emmerich, Hans Jaffé, Peter Schat, and Lech Tomaszewski; manuscript and documents related to Peter Mayer article "Practical Chemistry;" and photographs reproduced in Situationist Times nos. 4, 5, and 7. Situationist Times no. 7 was never published.

did.unittitle: Papers relating to Situationist Times nos. 6 and 7

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes original materials for Situationist Times no. 6, including nine original lithographs by Pierre Alechinsky, Jacqueline de Jong, Asger Jorn, Wilfredo Lam, Roberto Matta, Antonio Saura, Topor, Jan Voss; subscription form; distribution list; table for organizing the printed layout; list of press coverage; and original copy of the issue. Also includes photographs to be reproduced in the never-published Situationist Times no. 7 and correspondence with Rhodos, Thomas Rap, and contributors to Situationist Times no. 6.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscript for "SPUR Prozess;" manifesto and the flier "Avantgarde ist unerwünscht!"; original poster for exhibition SPUR: Bilder und Plastiken at Galerie van de Loo (1962); brochure "Unver Bindliche, Richt Linien; original issue of Anscheag (Nov. 1964) and texturen (1964), inscribed on the inside cover by Dieter Kunzelmann; and newspaper clippings. Also includes letters to de Jong, primarily from from Dieter Kunzelmann, including hand-collaged postcards, discussion of Second Situationist International, and a letter written on the back of Kennedy Broadside, but also from Frank Böckelmann, Lothar Fischer, Rodolphe Gasché, Dieter Kunzelmann, Helmut Sturm, and Hans-Peter Zimmer; Lothar Fischer and Heimrad Prem exhibition catalogue from Galerie van de Loo; and death announcement of Heimrad Prem and Urmel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters discussing Drakabygget (Second Situationist International); a packet of notes and letters by Jacqueline de Jong on "topologie"; letters to Situationist Bauhaus; Asger Jorn; notes in various languages; many exchanges with Noël Arnaud, including praise of the Situationist Times no. 1; and Situationist tarot game (H. C.Doets), as well as personal correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes financial and administrative records; press clippings related to Situationist Times nos. 4 and 5; ICA Bulletin no. 133 (Mar. 1964); notes regarding distribution of Situationist Times; correspondence with collectors, including Hans Sohm and H. Becke (1969–70); and correspondence with the Situationist Times publisher Rhodos regarding financial matters and distribution.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes ICA Bulletin 107 (Sept./Oct. 1960); postcard and letter from Guy Debord; and a postcard from Asger Jorn, Guy Debord, and Hans-Peter Zimmer inviting de Jong to the ICA meeting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a mock-up of Guy Debord's planned version of Situationist Times no. 1 (never used).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscripts by Jacqueline de Jong, including "Procession," "To the martyrs of the tractionists," "The structure for the control of the new techniques of conditions," "Lodgieters en Ambivalentie," among others; original drawings by de Jong; copy of ludus no. 2 (Feb. 1961); "The Vicious Circle—or the Trialectical Development in European Art," an explication by Jacqueline de Jong of Situationist Times nos. 3–5 (in multiple drafts); introduction to Situationist Times no. 5 by Jacqueline de Jong; handwritten text by Jacqueline de Jong for Asger Jorn's 1983 show at the Barbican.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Situationist International texts and tracts, particularly related to the expulsion of Gruppe SPUR, including "Flugblätter," "Interna," "Nicht Hinauslehnen."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Situationist International texts and tracts, including "Aux Poubelles de L'Histoire," "Déclaration sur les process contre l'internationale situationniste en allegmagne federale," "Hands off Alexander Trocchi" by Guy Debord; Lettrist International broadside.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes reprints of black-and-white photographs of Giuseppe Pinot-Gallizio with Jacqueline de Jong in his studio.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes texts of Mutant: drafts of Guy Debord and Asger Jorn, "European critique of the academic association of universities, colleges, and research institutes…." in French and translated into English; copy of original Mutant tract.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters and postcards between Guy Debord and Jacqueline de Jong.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photocopies of original documents related to the Situationist International exhibition at the Stedelijk Museum, Amsterdam (1960).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documents related to the Göteborg conference, including notes taken by Jacqueline de Jong; press clippings; programmatic outline.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material related to the periodical Drakabygget including brochures; flyer; and letters (on Situationist Bauhaus letterhead) and manuscripts by Jørgen Nash.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes personal and professional correspondence with Hans L. C. Jaffé; Françoise Coulon, director of Musée des Arts décoratifs; Hans-Peter Zimmer; Gordon Fazakerley; Jasia Reichardt of the Institute of Contemporary Arts, London; Robert Kennedy of Victoria & Albert Museum; Ernst Fischer; Paul de Lussanet; and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Jacqueline de Jong's parents, Knut Andersen (Norwegian filmmaker); Noël Arnaud; Guy Atkins; Niels Blaedel (Danish author); V. Blatter; André Blavier (Belgian poet); Pol Bury (Belgian sculptor); Erik Dal (Danish literary historian); Guy Debord; Folke Edwards (Danish curator and art critic); David Georges Emmerich (architect and contributor to Situationist Times); Gordon Fazakerley (abstract expressionist painter and contributor to Situationist Times); Doris Hasenfratz (German journalist and writer); Per Houdenakk (Norwegian art historian); Hans L. C. Jaffé (German art historian); Jorgen Klaus; John Lefevre (Lefevre Gallery, London); Lothar Fischer (sculptor and member of Gruppe SPUR); Dieter Kunzelmann (political activist and member of Gruppe SPUR); Paolo Marinotti (director of International Center for Arts and Customs, Venice); Peter Mayer (American painter); Rudolph Pereboom; Marc Pessin (French editor); Per Remfeldt; Virtus Schade (Danish journalist and writer); Piero Simondo (Italian artist); Maud Springer; Hans Sohm (collector); Le Tardy, Stefan and Franciszka Themerson (publishers of Gaberbocchus Press); Lech Tomaszewski (Polish architect); Otto van de Loo (German gallerist); Marion Watson; Richard Wilcocks, British artist); and Hans-Peter Zimmer (painter and sculptor, member of Gruppe SPUR).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with David Gestener, Kill Kleeman, Gert Mosler, Pem Slyters, Coen Stork, Hetty Chapon Heule, and Jacqueline de Jong's parents, Alice and Hans de Jong.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily consists of correspondence with galleries regarding exhibitions of de Jong's work, including Association Artistic Ascona, Switzerland; Galerie Delta, Rotterdam; Institut Néerlandais, Paris; Institute of Contemporary Art, London; Galerie Moderne, Silkeborg; Galerie Van de Loo, Munich; Westing Gallery, Odense; Galerie Gammel Strand, Copenhagen (1962–1974); letters from Noël Arnaud, Juan Carlos Aznar, André Balthazar, André Blavier, Michel Casse, Lourdes Castro, Gordon Fazakerly, Antoinette Momdon, Albert Niels, Paul Op de Coul, Paul Speck, Daniel Spoerri. Also includes invitation cards, brochures, and a copy of an article related to gallery exhibitions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Galerie du Dragon (July 1969) relating to Jacqueline de Jong's poster production, Galerie du Dragon and the Stedelijk Museum regarding the purchase of posters from the Paris 1968 uprisings for the Stedelijk Museum, Le Daily-Bul (a gallery in La Louvière), Norwegian art historian Per Houdenakk, Oslo Kunstforening, Bergens Kunstforening, Galerie Waalkens, Gallery Tardy, American Art Gallery, Gordon Fazakerley, Galerie Gammel Strand, and Erik Petersen.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Pierre Alechinsky, Peter Armitage, Guido Basi, Christian Boltanski, Pol Bury, Jan Cremer, Hans Haacke, Roberto Matta, Isamu Noguchi, Richard Nonas, Ad Petersen, Carlos Saura, Pierre Soulages, Eric Schumacher, Eduard van der Elsken, Jan Voss, Jan Vrijman, and Jaap Wagemaker. Also includes four photographs, including two of Daniel Spoerri and de Jong.

did.unittitle: Guy Atkins typescript, "New York Diary," with a letter to Asger Jorn
Correspondence to Jacqueline de Jong or Asger Jorn

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Marinus Anderse, Noël Arnaud, Gerard Franceschi, P.V. Glob, Arne Peterson, Virtus Schade, and Otto van de Loo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Galleries included in the correspondence primarily consist of Lefevre Gallery, but also J.J. Aberbach Gallery, Galerie Birch, and Galerie Van de Loo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscripts and typescripts, four drawings on tracing paper for The Scandinavian Institute, eight photographs, and 4 press clippings.

did.unittitle: Manuscript, "The Case of the Ascetic Satyr"
Correspondence, notes, original illustrations, and other materials

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Asger Jorn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles include, Le Pave , Le Monde , Le Figaro , L'engrage , Le Nouvel Observateur , Action , La Cause de Peuple , God, Nederland and Orange , Algemeen Handelsblad , Kontrast , and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscripts, writings, and Held og Hasard material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Projectes include Le Petit Livre/The Little Book, Dream Book, Recueil de notes et de croquis, Electron, Chanson du pouvoir inattendue, and Les aventure Vskaye ou les alouettes sur Ie canape.
